REPLY RE: MOTION BY DEFT FILED BY DEFENDANT MISSION LANGUAGE AND VOCATIONAL SCHOOL, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT RICARDO ALVA'S MOTION FOR STATUTORY ATTORNEY'S FEES IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF ADMITS THAT INDIVIDUAL EMPLOYEES, INCLUDING SUPERVISORS, CANNOT BE LIABLE FOR DISCRIMINATION CLAIMS UNDER FEHA AND TITLE VII. DESPITE THIS, PLAINTIFF SUED HIS SUPERVISOR IN HIS INDIVIDUAL CAPACITY AND DID NOT DISMISS HIM PRIOR TO THE JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS MOTION FILED BY DEFENDANT. THEREFORE, THE FEES IN THE AMOUNT OF $2150 ARE REASONABLE AND WARRANTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

REMOVED FROM MASTER JURY CALENDAR SET FOR OCT-09-2012 - ***CASE DISMISSED WITHOUT PREJUDICE.*** ELIZABETH MACDONALD SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. EDWARD GARCIA APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS. MISS MACDONALD REPRESENTED TO THE COURT THAT PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL, DANIEL MITCHELL, IS IN TRIAL IN ALAMEDA COUNTY AND WOULD LIKE TO TRAIL. THE COURT DENIED PLAINTIFF'S EX PARTE TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL FILED ON 10/5/12 BECAUSE THERE WAS NO STIPULATION AND THERE WERE THREE PREVIOUS CONTINUANCES OF THE TRIAL DATE AND IT WAS SPECIFIED ON THE PREVIOUS ORDER THAT ALL FURTHER REQUESTS COULD BE MADE BY REGULARLY NOTICED MOTION ONLY WHICH WAS NOT DONE. THE COURT PASSED THE MATTER FOR MISS MACDONALD TO CONTACT MR. MITCHELL AND THE PLAINTIFF ROBERTO VELASQUEZ. AT AROUND 10:20AM, THE MATTER WAS RECALLED AND MISS MACDONALD WAS UNABLE TO REACH MR. MITCHELL. MISS MACDONALD IS NOT READY TO PROCEED TO GO TO TRIAL AND PLAINTIFF ROBERTO VELASQUEZ IS NOT PRESENT IN COURT. THE COURT ORDERED THE CASE DISMISSED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. JUDGE: KATHERINE FEINSTEIN; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - DEFENDANT GRANILLO'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AN AMENDED ANSWER IS GRANTED. Order signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

Notice Of Motion For Statutory Attorney'S Fees
Off Calendar Jun-29-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT RICARDO ALVA'S MOTION FOR STATUTORY ATTORNEY'S FEES.
Appearances: Edward Garcia, Jr. of the Law Offices of Bragg & Kuluva (273.6500) for defendants and moving parties; No other appearances.
The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: defendant Alva's motion for statutory attorney's fees is granted. Plaintiff admits that individual employees, including supervisors, cannot be liable for discrimination claims under FEHA and Title VII. Despite this, plaintiff sued his supervisor in his individual capacity and did not dismiss him prior to the judgment on the pleadings motion filed by defendant. Therefore, the fees in the amount of $2150 are reasonable and warranted. Order signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
